name: LaunchDarkly
what_it_does: Manages feature flag lifecycle and targeting rules via REST API, while SDKs evaluate flags locally using streamed rule sets so agents can toggle features without per-evaluation API calls.
use_cases: Toggle a feature flag on or off for a specific environment via API during an automated incident rollback workflow
Create a new feature flag with targeting rules when provisioning a canary deployment in CI/CD
Update percentage rollout on an existing flag to gradually increase traffic to a new code path based on error rate thresholds
List all flags enabled for a given environment to generate a feature inventory report for compliance auditing
Archive stale flags older than 90 days with zero evaluation counts to reduce flag debt automatically
not_for: Error monitoring or crash reporting (use Sentry or Bugsnag instead)
A/B test statistical analysis with warehouse-native computation (use GrowthBook or Statsig)
Real-time application performance tracing and APM (use Datadog or New Relic)
best_when: An agent needs programmatic control over feature rollouts in production with millisecond SDK evaluation latency and enterprise-grade targeting rules.
avoid_when: The team needs a self-hosted open-source solution or the budget does not support per-seat SaaS pricing at scale.

auth.notes: Access tokens with role-based permissions (Reader, Writer, Admin). Personal and service tokens supported. OAuth2 available for third-party integrations via LaunchDarkly App Directory.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1,000 MAU (Monthly Active Users), 3 team members, unlimited flags, 1 environment
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter ~$12/month per seat; Pro and Enterprise tiers with experimentation, approvals, and SSO
pricing.notes: Pricing based on MAU and seats. SDK evaluation does not consume API quota — only management API calls count against rate limits.

performance.rate_limits: Management REST API: 10 requests/second per token; SDK streaming connections: no per-evaluation API calls; webhook delivery best-effort

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields. OpenAPI spec is accurate and machine-readable. 409 conflicts include diff of conflicting state.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: PATCH operations using semantic patches are idempotent for most flag mutations. Flag creation (POST) is not idempotent — duplicate POSTs create duplicate flags if key differs.
known_agent_gotchas: LaunchDarkly uses semantic patch (application/json; domain-model=launchdarkly.semanticpatch) not standard JSON Patch (RFC 6902) — sending standard patch format returns 415 with a confusing error
SDK evaluation happens client-side from a streamed ruleset; the REST API is only for management, not per-flag evaluation — agents that call the API per flag check add unnecessary latency and cost
Environments are separate API resources with their own keys; a flag may have different values per environment and requests scoped to the wrong environment silently return wrong values
Rate limit is 10 req/s which is easy to saturate when iterating over large flag lists — the API does not return Retry-After headers, only 429 status
Flag keys are immutable after creation; if an agent creates a flag with the wrong key it must be archived and recreated, it cannot be renamed

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP Moderate authorized. HIPAA BAA available. EU data residency. Fine-grained RBAC with custom roles on Enterprise. SDK keys are read-only — cannot modify flags.
